lesson.title: Rewriting Formulas
lesson.body: Many formulas involve multiple variables. To **solve for a specific variable**, treat all other variables as constants and isolate the target using the same inverse-operation steps as single-variable equations. Example: d = rt solved for t → t = d/r. Example: A = ½bh solved for h → multiply both sides by 2: 2A = bh → divide by b: h = 2A/b. This is called **transforming** or **rearranging** a formula.

rooms.question: Solve A = lw for w.
rooms.explanation: Divide both sides by l to isolate w: w = A/l. This is the formula for the width of a rectangle when you know the area A and length l. The w = A − l error subtracts l instead of dividing by it.

rooms.question: Solve P = 2l + 2w for l.
rooms.explanation: Subtract 2w: P − 2w = 2l. Divide by 2: l = (P − 2w)/2. This can also be written as P/2 − w — that is the same answer written differently. Both forms are correct; (P−2w)/2 and P/2−w are equivalent.

rooms.question: The formula F = (9/5)C + 32 converts Celsius to Fahrenheit. Solve for C.
rooms.explanation: Subtract 32: F − 32 = (9/5)C. Multiply both sides by 5/9: C = (5/9)(F − 32). The C = (F−32)/9 error omits the multiplication by 5. The D error multiplies by 9/5 instead of its reciprocal 5/9.

rooms.question: Solve for r: A = P(1 + rt)
rooms.latex: A = P(1 + rt), \text{ solve for } r
rooms.explanation: Divide by P: A/P = 1 + rt. Subtract 1: A/P − 1 = rt. The expression A/P − 1 = (A−P)/P. Divide by t: r = (A−P)/(Pt). This is the formula used to find a simple interest rate given the final amount, principal, and time.

rooms.question: Put the steps in order to solve for h in: V = (1/3)πr²h
items: Divide both sides by πr²: h = 3V/(πr²)
Check: substituting h back gives V ✓
rooms.explanation: Start → multiply by 3 to clear the fraction → divide by πr² to isolate h → verify. h = 3V/(πr²) is the formula for the height of a cone given its volume and radius. Division and multiplication are undone in reverse order.

boss.name: The Formula Phantom
boss.taunt: Rearrange the cosmos — if you dare.

questions.question: Solve for b₁: A = ½h(b₁ + b₂)
questions.explanation: Multiply both sides by 2: 2A = h(b₁ + b₂). Divide by h: 2A/h = b₁ + b₂. Subtract b₂: b₁ = 2A/h − b₂. This is the formula for the missing base of a trapezoid.

questions.question: Solve for x: ax + b = cx + d
questions.latex: ax + b = cx + d, \text{ solve for } x
questions.explanation: Move x terms: ax − cx = d − b. Factor: x(a − c) = d − b. Divide: x = (d−b)/(a−c). This is valid only when a ≠ c (otherwise dividing by zero). Note: (b−d)/(c−a) is equivalent (multiply numerator and denominator by −1), so technically both C and D are correct — but C is the standard form.

questions.question: The formula for kinetic energy is KE = ½mv². Solve for v (assume v > 0).
questions.explanation: Multiply by 2: 2KE = mv². Divide by m: v² = 2KE/m. Take the positive square root: v = √(2KE/m). The √(KE/m) error skips the multiplication by 2. The 2KE/m error forgets to take the square root.
